parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces a new retry subsystem (retry.mjs, 203 lines), error dispatch layer (bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines), and significant changes to the API client (glm-client.mjs, 155 lines) and job tracking (tracked-jobs.mjs, 78 lines) — yet three of those four core modules have no dedicated test files. The deleted process.mjs (67 lines removed) creates a latent import-failure risk. Shipping without test coverage on the primary HTTP client and job-state machinery is a regression hazard under retry/backoff stress.

findings.title: No test coverage for glm-client.mjs despite 155 lines of auth/retry/error changes
findings.body: glm-client.mjs is the primary API communication module. It received ~155 lines of changes across v0.4.x integrating the new retry.mjs backoff, bigmodel-errors.mjs error dispatch, and likely auth/connection handling. There is no corresponding test file (tests/glm-client.test.mjs is absent from the test listing). This means the retry-integration path, error-classification fallback, and auth-failure recovery are all untested. Under degraded-network or rate-limit conditions, a misclassified error or botched retry could silently swallow a job result or enter an infinite-retry loop with no test to catch it.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering at minimum: (1) retry exhaustion propagates a distinguishable error, (2) BigModel error classification maps to correct retry/backoff strategy, (3) transient auth failures retry correctly, (4) non-retryable errors fail fast without retries.

findings.title: No test coverage for tracked-jobs.mjs — job state corruption risk under concurrency
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manages job lifecycle state, likely persisted to the filesystem. File-based state with no dedicated test file (tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs is absent) means race conditions between concurrent job create/complete/fail transitions are untested. A partially written state file or crash mid-update could leave jobs in an unrecoverable state with no automated guard against it.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: (1) concurrent job create + complete does not corrupt state, (2) crash-recovery scenario where state file is partially written, (3) job lifecycle transitions enforce valid state machine ordering.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted (67 lines) — dangling import risk if callers not fully migrated
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely removed (67 ---, 0 +++). scripts/check-imports.mjs was updated (5 lines changed), suggesting awareness of the migration. However, if any runtime code path still imports from './lib/process.mjs' or if the import checker has gaps (e.g., dynamic imports, re-exports, or string-concatenated paths), it would produce an ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime with no compile-time warning in an ESM-only project.
findings.recommendation: Run a full grep for 'process' across all .mjs files (including dynamic import patterns and template literals) to confirm no dangling references. Add a CI check that validates all static imports resolve before merge.

findings.title: No test coverage for job-control.mjs — retry/interrupt interaction untested
findings.body: scripts/lib/job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) controls job execution flow and likely interacts with the new retry.mjs subsystem. Without a dedicated test (tests/job-control.test.mjs absent), the interaction between job cancellation, retry backoff, and state cleanup is unverified. A retry that fires after job cancellation could write stale state or re-queue a dead job.
findings.recommendation: Add tests for job-control.mjs specifically testing: (1) retry does not fire after explicit job cancellation, (2) interrupted jobs clean up state correctly, (3) concurrent cancel + retry does not leave ghost jobs.

next_steps: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs with retry-exhaustion, error-classification, and auth-failure scenarios before shipping.
Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs with concurrent-access and crash-recovery scenarios.
Grep all .mjs files for any remaining import of or reference to scripts/lib/process.mjs (including dynamic imports).
Run the full test suite under simulated network degradation to verify retry.mjs integration with glm-client.mjs behaves correctly at the boundary.
Verify that the retry subsystem has a hard ceiling on total retry duration and cannot exceed reasonable resource limits under sustained failure.
